prompt: The equals() override is inconsistent with hashCode(). What is the bug?
choices: HashSet cannot store objects without a default constructor
equals() is overridden but hashCode() is not; equal Points hash to different buckets, so HashSet.contains() returns false even for logically equal objects
The instanceof check should use Point.class.isInstance(o) instead
set.contains() requires the Point to be the exact same object reference
explanation: The equals/hashCode contract requires equal objects to have the same hash code. Without overriding hashCode(), two equal Points (same x,y) produce different hash codes (default identity hash), so they land in different buckets. HashSet.contains() finds false. Fix: override hashCode(): return Objects.hash(x, y);
